posts.content: Bought a new Mac mini to properly tinker with claws over the weekend. The apple store person told me they are selling like hotcakes and everyone is confused :) I'm definitely a bit sus'd to run OpenClaw specifically - giving my private data/keys to 400K lines of vibe coded
posts.highlights: Karpathy bought a Mac mini to experiment with 'claws' (likely a typo for 'Claude' or 'Claw' agent), noting that Apple Store staff said they are selling well and customers are confused. He is cautious about running OpenClaw due to security concerns with vibe-coded code.

posts.content: im excited about agent harnesses because i think are the first stable agent abstractions we can build on top (which is why we're investing so much in deepagents) we always wanted to run llms in a loop and have them call tools (remember autoGPT? that's all that was) but the
posts.highlights: Agent harnesses provide stable abstractions for building agent loops with tool calling, a key evolution from early attempts like AutoGPT.

posts.content: This means that operations you would do on code in the software world, you now do on traces in the agent world. Debugging, testing, profiling

posts.content: TL;DR: More and more agents need a workspace: a computer where they can run code, install packages, and access files. Sandboxes provide this
